domains.title: Begin Here
domains.intro: How to think when you have nothing, and how to use this guide as a ladder from the first night to a working village.

guides.title: How to use this guide
guides.summary: See how every guide unlocks the next like branches of a tree, and learn to pick the right next step for where you are.

guides.title: What matters first
guides.summary: You can live weeks without food but only hours in bad cold, so fix things in this order: shelter, water, fire, food.

guides.title: Stop, breathe, take stock
guides.summary: Slow your breathing, check what you have and what hurts, and make a simple plan before doing anything else.

guides.title: Spot danger before it finds you
guides.summary: Learn to stay away from flood channels, dead hanging branches, cliff edges, and animal dens.

guides.title: Help rescuers find you
guides.summary: Stay in one place, make big bright marks, and use sound and shine so searching people can spot you.

zh.title: 从这里开始
zh.intro: 一无所有时该怎么想，以及怎么把这本指南当作梯子，从第一夜一步步爬到一座能运转的村庄。

es.title: Empieza aquí
es.intro: Cómo pensar cuando no tienes nada, y cómo usar esta guía como una escalera: de la primera noche a una aldea que funciona.

yue.title: 由呢度開始
yue.intro: 咩都冇嘅時候點樣諗，同埋點樣用呢本指南做梯子，由第一晚一步步爬到一條行得順嘅村。

domains.title: The First Night
domains.intro: Stay warm, dry, and unlost through the first nights, before you own a single tool.

guides.title: Find shelter fast
guides.summary: Use what is already there — thick trees, rock overhangs, dry hollows — to get out of the wind and rain.

guides.title: Stay warm without fire
guides.summary: Stuff dry leaves and grass around your body and get off the cold ground so your own heat keeps you warm.

guides.title: Build a leaf hut for the night
guides.summary: Lean sticks against a ridge pole and pile leaves on thick until it is a warm nest you can crawl into.

guides.title: Find water
guides.summary: Follow low ground, green plants, and animal trails downhill until you reach a stream, spring, or pool.

guides.title: Read the sky
guides.summary: Watch clouds, wind, and animals to tell if rain or cold is coming before it arrives.

guides.title: Find your way
guides.summary: Use the sun by day and the stars by night so you can leave camp and always get back.

zh.title: 第一夜
zh.intro: 在你还没有任何工具之前，先熬过最初几夜：不冷、不湿、不迷路。

es.title: La primera noche
es.intro: Pasa las primeras noches caliente, seco y sin perderte, antes de tener una sola herramienta.

yue.title: 第一晚
yue.intro: 喺你未有任何工具之前，先捱過最初嗰幾晚：唔凍、唔濕、唔蕩失路。

domains.title: First Tools
domains.intro: Sharp edges, string, knots, and handles — the small things every other craft is built on.

guides.title: Make sharp tools from stone
guides.summary: Break the right rocks the right way to get cutting edges, scrapers, and hammers.

guides.title: Make needles and hooks from bone
guides.summary: Split and grind bones into needles, fish hooks, and hole-pokers that stone cannot match.

guides.title: Twist plants into string and rope
guides.summary: Roll and twist bark, nettle, or grass fibers into cord strong enough to trust your work to.

guides.title: Tie knots and lashings that hold
guides.summary: Learn a small set of knots for tying things down, binding poles into frames, and hanging things up.

guides.title: Weave a basket
guides.summary: Bend fresh green shoots over and under each other until they hold a shape that carries things.

guides.title: Carve wooden tools
guides.summary: Use your stone edge to make a digging stick, a mallet, a spoon, and pegs.

guides.title: Put a handle on a stone head
guides.summary: Notch, lash, and wedge a stone blade onto a wooden handle to make an axe you can really swing.

guides.title: Sharpen and care for your tools
guides.summary: Grind edges on a wet stone and keep wood and cord dry so your tools outlast the seasons.

zh.title: 最初的工具
zh.intro: 锋利的刃口、绳子、绳结和手柄——其他所有手艺都建立在这些小东西上。

es.title: Primeras herramientas
es.intro: Filos, cuerda, nudos y mangos: las cosas pequeñas sobre las que se construye todo lo demás.

yue.title: 最初嘅工具
yue.intro: 鋒利嘅刀口、繩、繩結同手柄——之後所有手藝都係砌喺呢啲小嘢上面。

domains.title: Fire
domains.intro: Make fire, keep it alive, and put it to work — the single biggest branch point in the whole tree.

guides.title: Gather tinder and firewood
guides.summary: Collect the fluffy dry stuff that catches a spark, and sort wood from thin sticks to thick logs.

guides.title: Make a safe fire pit and lay a fire
guides.summary: Clear the ground, ring it with stone or bare earth, stack tinder and sticks so a tiny flame can grow, and know how you will put it out.

guides.title: Start a fire with sparks
guides.summary: Strike sparks from hard stone and steel into your tinder and blow the glow gently into flame.

guides.title: Make fire with a bow drill
guides.summary: Spin a wooden stick with a small bow until the rubbing makes a glowing ember, then breathe it into flame.

guides.title: Keep a fire day and night
guides.summary: Bank the coals at night, wake them in the morning, and carry a wrapped ember to a new camp so you never start from zero.

guides.title: Cook food over fire
guides.summary: Roast food on sticks and hot stones so it is safe to eat and tastes good.

guides.title: Cook in a pit oven
guides.summary: Bury food with fire-hot stones and let the ground bake it slow and even while you work.

guides.title: Make charcoal
guides.summary: Cook wood slowly with almost no air so it becomes light black fuel that burns far hotter than wood — the key to lime, glass, and metal.

zh.title: 火
zh.intro: 生火、养火、用火——整棵树上最重要的分岔点。

es.title: Fuego
es.intro: Haz fuego, mantenlo vivo y ponlo a trabajar: la rama más importante de todo el árbol.

yue.title: 火
yue.intro: 生火、養火、用火——成棵樹入面最重要嘅分岔位。

domains.title: Safe Water and Wild Food
domains.intro: Drink without getting sick and eat from the wild while your garden is still a dream.

guides.title: Boil water to make it safe
guides.summary: Bring water to a rolling boil — even clear water can carry sickness you cannot see.

guides.title: Make a charcoal and sand filter
guides.summary: Layer cloth, sand, and crushed charcoal so cloudy water runs out clear — then boil it anyway.

guides.title: Carry and store water
guides.summary: Turn gourds, bark, and hollow wood into bottles, catch rain when it falls, and keep stored water covered and clean.

guides.title: Pick wild plants without being poisoned
guides.summary: Learn the iron rule that keeps foragers alive: never eat anything you cannot surely name.

guides.title: Find the easy wild foods
guides.summary: Gather the nuts, berries, roots, and greens that are hard to mistake for anything bad, and learn what ripens when.

guides.title: Gather eggs, shellfish, and insects
guides.summary: Collect the small easy proteins most people walk right past, and cook them well.

guides.title: Catch fish with lines and traps
guides.summary: Make a hook from bone or thorn for a hand line, and build a woven funnel trap that fishes while you sleep.

guides.title: Set a snare for small animals
guides.summary: Hang a small cord loop on an animal trail, and check it often so nothing suffers.

guides.title: Clean and cook your catch
guides.summary: Gut and skin a fish or small animal, waste nothing, and cook it all the way through.

zh.title: 净水与野食
zh.intro: 喝水不生病；菜园还只是个梦的时候，先靠野外吃饱。

es.title: Agua segura y comida silvestre
es.intro: Bebe sin enfermarte y come del campo mientras tu huerto sigue siendo un sueño.

yue.title: 乾淨水同野外食物
yue.intro: 飲水唔會病；菜園仲係一個夢嘅時候，先靠山野食飽。

domains.title: Clay and Kilns
domains.intro: Sticky earth plus fire gives you pots to boil in, bricks to build with, and kilns hot enough for everything that comes later.

guides.title: Find and clean clay
guides.summary: Dig sticky earth from stream banks, test it with a squeeze and a roll, and knead out the stones and air.

guides.title: Shape pots by pinch and coil
guides.summary: Pinch a ball of clay into a small bowl, then roll clay snakes and stack them into pots big enough to cook in.

guides.title: Fire pots in an open pit
guides.summary: Dry your pots slowly, then bake them in a hot pit so soft clay turns hard as stone.

guides.title: Cook and boil in clay pots
guides.summary: At last: soup, stew, and easy safe water straight over the fire.

guides.title: Build a simple kiln
guides.summary: Build a clay oven with a fire hole and a draft so pots fire harder and fewer of them crack.

guides.title: Make mud bricks and fired bricks
guides.summary: Press clay and straw into molds, dry the blocks in the sun, and fire the best ones hard in the kiln.

guides.title: Build a bread oven
guides.summary: Shape a clay dome that soaks up fire and bakes with its stored heat.

guides.title: Spin a potter's wheel
guides.summary: A heavy spinning disc lets you pull up round, even pots in minutes instead of hours.

zh.title: 陶土与窑
zh.intro: 黏土加上火，就有了能煮饭的锅、能盖房的砖，和之后一切所需的高温窑。

es.title: Arcilla y hornos
es.intro: Tierra pegajosa más fuego: ollas para hervir, ladrillos para construir y hornos con calor para todo lo que viene después.

yue.title: 泥同窯
yue.intro: 黏土加火，就有煮飯嘅煲、起屋嘅磚，同埋之後樣樣都要用嘅高溫窯。

domains.title: Keeping Food
domains.intro: Salt, smoke, sourness, and cool dark places make the good harvest days feed the empty winter days.

guides.title: Dry food in sun and wind
guides.summary: Slice food thin and let moving air take out the water that rot needs to live.

guides.title: Smoke fish and meat
guides.summary: Salt-cure fish and meat, then hang them in cool smoke for two days until they dry hard and keep for weeks.

guides.title: Make salt
guides.summary: Boil seawater or salty spring water down and down until white salt crystals are left in the pot.

guides.title: Ferment vegetables in brine
guides.summary: Pack vegetables with salt under their own juice and let a friendly sourness keep them safe all winter.

guides.title: Make vinegar
guides.summary: Let fruit juice ferment and then sour in open air into vinegar for keeping food and cleaning.

guides.title: Render and store fat
guides.summary: Melt fat down slow and clean so it keeps for cooking, for lamps, and for soap later.

guides.title: Dig a cool food store
guides.summary: Store roots, fruits, and jars in the cool steady earth so they last for months instead of days.

zh.title: 存住食物
zh.intro: 盐、烟、酸味和阴凉的地窖，让丰收的日子喂饱挨饿的冬天。

es.title: Conservar la comida
es.intro: Sal, humo, acidez y lugares frescos y oscuros hacen que los días de buena cosecha alimenten los días vacíos del invierno.

yue.title: 留得住嘅食物
yue.intro: 鹽、煙、酸味同陰涼嘅地窖，令豐收嘅日子養得起捱餓嘅冬天。

domains.title: A Home That Lasts
domains.intro: Wood, mud, stone, and thatch come together into a dry, warm house with a fire that stays where it belongs.

guides.title: Choose where to build
guides.summary: Pick ground that is dry, safe from floods and falling trees, facing the sun, and near water but not too near.

guides.title: Move heavy things with levers, ramps, and rollers
guides.summary: A long pole over a stone, a gentle slope, and logs under the load let one person move what ten could not lift.

guides.title: Fell trees and split planks
guides.summary: Cut small trees safely, then hammer wooden wedges along the grain to split out posts and flat boards without a saw.

guides.title: Raise a wooden frame
guides.summary: Cut, notch, lash, and peg posts and beams into a strong skeleton that will carry the roof.

guides.title: Weave and mud the walls
guides.summary: Weave springy branches between the posts and press on mud, straw, and dung until the wind cannot find a way in.

guides.title: Thatch a roof
guides.summary: Tie bundles of reeds or straw in overlapping rows, layered like a bird's feathers, so rain runs off for years.

guides.title: Build a hearth and chimney
guides.summary: Make a safe brick-and-stone place for fire inside, with a way for smoke to leave without you leaving too.

guides.title: Fit a door and make simple furniture
guides.summary: Hang a plank door that shuts, build a bed off the cold ground, and make a stool and shelf to live with.

guides.title: Raise a one-room house
guides.summary: Put it all together — frame, woven walls, mud plaster, thatch, door, and hearth — into a home.

zh.title: 一座耐久的房子
zh.intro: 木头、泥巴、石头和茅草，合起来就是一间干燥暖和的房子，火乖乖待在该待的地方。

es.title: Una casa que dura
es.intro: Madera, barro, piedra y paja se unen en una casa seca y cálida, con un fuego que se queda donde debe.

yue.title: 一間住得耐嘅屋
yue.intro: 木、泥、石同茅草，夾埋就係一間又乾又暖嘅屋，火乖乖留喺佢應該喺嘅地方。

domains.title: Staying Well
domains.intro: Stop sickness before it starts, handle hurts calmly, and know exactly when to go find real medical care.

guides.title: Wash hands and keep clean
guides.summary: Wash hands with clean water before food and after the latrine — the single habit that stops most sickness.

guides.title: Dig a latrine far from water
guides.summary: Put body waste in a covered pit downhill and far away from any water or food.

guides.title: Clean and cover cuts
guides.summary: Wash a wound well with clean water, cover it, watch for redness, and get real medical help for deep ones.

guides.title: Handle burns, breaks, and sprains
guides.summary: Cool burns with clean water, splint broken bones straight and still, rest sprains — and get the hurt person to real care.

guides.title: Care for fever and gut sickness
guides.summary: Give rest and lots of safe water with a pinch of salt and sweet, and learn the danger signs that mean go for help now.

guides.title: Stop sickness from spreading
guides.summary: When someone is ill, let them rest apart, wash often, share no cups, and keep their water and food separate.

guides.title: Care for your teeth
guides.summary: Clean teeth every day with chew sticks and rinses, because teeth do not grow back.

guides.title: Know when to go for real help
guides.summary: Learn the signs that home care is not enough and reaching a doctor matters more than anything else in this book.

zh.title: 保持健康
zh.intro: 让病没机会开始，冷静处理伤痛，并且清楚什么时候必须去找真正的医生。

es.title: Mantenerse sano
es.intro: Detén la enfermedad antes de que empiece, atiende las heridas con calma y sabe exactamente cuándo buscar atención médica real.

yue.title: 身體健健康康
yue.intro: 唔俾病有機會開始，冷靜咁處理傷痛，仲要清楚幾時一定要搵真正嘅醫生。

domains.title: Growing Food
domains.intro: Stop chasing food every day and turn a patch of ground into food that comes back every year.

guides.title: Learn your seasons
guides.summary: Watch a full year — frost, rain, heat — so you know when to plant and when to store.

guides.title: Choose ground and dig garden beds
guides.summary: Find a sunny patch with dark crumbly soil and water close by, then loosen it deep so roots and rain can get in.

guides.title: Make compost
guides.summary: Pile green stuff, brown stuff, and dung, keep it damp, and let it rot into rich dark food for the soil.

guides.title: Plant seeds and keep them alive
guides.summary: Sow at the right depth and time, keep weeds out and water in, and guard young plants from hungry animals.

guides.title: Save seeds for next year
guides.summary: Let your best plants ripen fully, dry their seeds well, and store them cool and dry — that jar is next year's food.

guides.title: Grow a patch of grain
guides.summary: Sow wheat, barley, or corn thick in a guarded field — the food that stores all through winter.

guides.title: Harvest, thresh, and store grain
guides.summary: Cut the ripe grain, beat the seeds free, let the wind blow the husks away, and store it dry away from mice.

guides.title: Grind flour and bake bread
guides.summary: Rub grain between two stones into flour, catch wild yeast in a jar, and bake your first real loaf in the clay oven.

zh.title: 种出粮食
zh.intro: 别再天天找吃的——把一块地变成年年长回来的粮食。

es.title: Cultivar comida
es.intro: Deja de perseguir comida cada día y convierte un pedazo de tierra en alimento que vuelve cada año.

yue.title: 種得出糧食
yue.intro: 唔使日日撲食——將一塊地變成年年都長返出嚟嘅糧食。

domains.title: Keeping Animals
domains.intro: Eggs, milk, wool, and honey — food and materials that walk to you.

guides.title: Build pens and fences
guides.summary: Set posts and weave or rail between them so your animals stay in and hungry visitors stay out.

guides.title: Keep chickens
guides.summary: Build a coop that foxes cannot open, feed the birds your scraps, and collect eggs almost every day.

guides.title: Keep goats or sheep
guides.summary: Raise the tough animals that turn thorny brush into milk, wool, hides, and more animals.

guides.title: Milk an animal
guides.summary: With clean hands, a calm animal, and a clean pot, get fresh milk morning and evening.

guides.title: Make butter and simple cheese
guides.summary: Shake cream until it turns to butter, and let milk sour and drain into soft cheese that keeps longer than milk.

guides.title: Keep bees for honey
guides.summary: Give a wild swarm a good home and take a fair share of honey and wax without harming them.

guides.title: Keep animals healthy and raise their young
guides.summary: Give clean water, dry bedding, and a daily look-over, spot one sick animal before the whole flock is, and help at births when needed.

zh.title: 养起牲口
zh.intro: 蛋、奶、羊毛和蜂蜜——会自己走到你跟前的食物和材料。

es.title: Criar animales
es.intro: Huevos, leche, lana y miel: comida y materiales que caminan hacia ti.

yue.title: 養埋啲牲口
yue.intro: 蛋、奶、羊毛同蜂蜜——識自己行埋嚟嘅食物同材料。

domains.title: Everyday Chemistry
domains.intro: Ash, fat, bark, limestone, and sand — with fire, ordinary things transform into soap, glue, mortar, leather, light, and glass.

guides.title: Make glue and pitch
guides.summary: Melt tree resin with charcoal dust into a strong glue that sets hard and a black pitch that seals leaks.

guides.title: Make lye from wood ash
guides.summary: Drip water slowly through hardwood ash to draw out a strong cleaning liquid — handle it with care, it bites skin.

guides.title: Make soap from fat and lye
guides.summary: Cook fat and lye together — carefully — until they become the soap that keeps your whole village healthier.
guides.time: two days, plus weeks of curing

guides.title: Burn limestone into lime
guides.summary: Roast crushed limestone or seashells white-hot in the kiln, then wake the lime carefully with water — it gets hot and it bites.

guides.title: Mix mortar, plaster, and whitewash
guides.summary: Blend lime with sand and water to glue stones together, seal walls smooth, and paint rooms bright and clean.

guides.title: Tan a hide into leather
guides.summary: Scrape a skin clean, soak it in crushed-bark water for weeks, and work it soft into leather that lasts years.

guides.title: Make lamps and candles
guides.summary: Float a wick in a dish of fat, or dip a wick again and again into melted fat or beeswax, and push back the dark.

guides.title: Melt sand into glass
guides.summary: In your very hottest furnace, sand with plant ash and lime melts into glass for beads and small vessels.

zh.title: 日常化学
zh.intro: 草木灰、油脂、树皮、石灰石和沙子——遇上火，普通东西就变成肥皂、胶、灰浆、皮革、灯光和玻璃。

es.title: Química de todos los días
es.intro: Ceniza, grasa, corteza, caliza y arena: con fuego, lo ordinario se transforma en jabón, pegamento, mortero, cuero, luz y vidrio.

yue.title: 日常化學
yue.intro: 火灰、油脂、樹皮、石灰石同沙——遇上火，平平無奇嘅嘢就變成番梘、膠水、灰漿、皮革、燈光同玻璃。

domains.title: Thread and Cloth
domains.intro: From plant stems and sheep's backs to thread, cloth, color, and clothes that keep the weather out.

guides.title: Free fibers from plants
guides.summary: Soak nettle or flax stems in water until the woody parts rot away and the long strong fibers come free.

guides.title: Get wool ready to spin
guides.summary: Shear a sheep or comb a goat, wash the wool, and comb it fluffy and straight.

guides.title: Spin thread with a drop spindle
guides.summary: Twirl a weighted stick so it twists wool or plant fiber into long, even thread while you walk or talk.

guides.title: Weave cloth on a simple loom
guides.summary: Stretch threads tight on a frame and weave over and under until real cloth grows row by row under your hands.

guides.title: Make felt from wool
guides.summary: Rub wet soapy wool until it tangles into thick warm fabric for hats, boots, and blankets — no weaving needed.

guides.title: Make a needle and sew clothes
guides.summary: Stitch with a bone needle, then cut cloth into simple shapes and sew a tunic, a wrap, and a cloak that fit.

guides.title: Dye cloth with plants
guides.summary: Simmer onion skins, roots, and bark with a fixer so cloth takes color that stays through washing.

guides.title: Make shoes and bags from leather
guides.summary: Cut, punch, and stitch leather into the shoes, belts, and bags that outlast cloth.

zh.title: 线与布
zh.intro: 从植物茎秆和羊背上的毛，到线、布、颜色和挡风遮雨的衣服。

es.title: Hilo y tela
es.intro: De tallos de plantas y lomos de oveja a hilo, tela, color y ropa que deja el clima afuera.

yue.title: 線同布
yue.intro: 由植物莖稈同羊背上嘅毛，變成線、布、顏色，同埋擋風擋雨嘅衫。

domains.title: Metal
domains.intro: Charcoal, bellows, and a clay furnace pull metal out of rock — the hardest and most rewarding climb in the book.

guides.title: Find metal in rocks
guides.summary: Learn the heavy, oddly colored rocks — green-stained copper, rusty red iron, black stream sand — and where they hide.

guides.title: Make bellows
guides.summary: Sew a leather-and-wood air pump, because metal-melting fire must breathe harder than lungs can blow.

guides.title: Build a smelting furnace
guides.summary: Stack a clay chimney-furnace that holds charcoal and ore while the bellows drive the heat higher than any campfire.

guides.title: Smelt and cast copper
guides.summary: Roast green-stained ore with charcoal until beads of shining metal form, then pour melted copper into clay molds.

guides.title: Smelt iron in a bloomery
guides.summary: Feed ore and charcoal into the furnace for hours of pumping to win a glowing spongy lump of real iron.

guides.title: Hammer hot iron
guides.summary: With an anvil stone, tongs, and a hammer, glowing iron moves like stiff clay under your blows.

guides.title: Forge nails, hooks, hinges, and a knife
guides.summary: Hammer out the small everyday iron things that make every other job in this book easier.

guides.title: Harden and temper a blade
guides.summary: Heat, quench, and gently re-warm iron so an edge stays sharp without snapping.

zh.title: 金属
zh.intro: 木炭、风箱和一座黏土炉，把金属从石头里拽出来——全书最难爬、也最值得爬的一段。

es.title: Metal
es.intro: Carbón, fuelles y un horno de arcilla sacan el metal de la roca: la subida más dura y más gratificante del libro.

yue.title: 金屬
yue.intro: 木炭、風箱同一座黏土爐，將金屬由石頭入面拉出嚟——成本書最難爬、又最抵爬嗰段。

domains.title: Paths, Wheels and Water
domains.intro: Sleds, bridges, wheels, boats, and a wheel the stream turns for you — how to move more than your own back can carry.

guides.title: Build a sled to drag heavy loads
guides.summary: Two smooth runners and a lashed frame let you drag what you could never lift.

guides.title: Lift heavy things with ropes and poles
guides.summary: A tripod of poles and a good rope let one person raise a beam or pull a log out of a river.

guides.title: Lay paths and small bridges
guides.summary: Firm dry paths and a safe log bridge tie your fields, water, and neighbors together.

guides.title: Make a wheel and axle
guides.summary: Build a solid wooden wheel that turns true — the shape that carries civilisation.

guides.title: Build a hand barrow or cart
guides.summary: One wheel, two handles, and a frame move a whole harvest with half the work.

guides.title: Lash a raft
guides.summary: Dry logs and good knots make a raft that carries you and your load across calm water.

guides.title: Burn and carve a dugout canoe
guides.summary: Hollow a single log with careful fire and a scraper until it floats light and true.

guides.title: Build a water wheel
guides.summary: Let the stream turn a wheel that grinds grain or lifts water — the first worker that never tires.

zh.title: 路、轮子和水
zh.intro: 雪橇、桥、轮子、船，还有溪水替你转的水轮——搬动远超你脊背能扛的东西。

es.title: Caminos, ruedas y agua
es.intro: Trineos, puentes, ruedas, botes y una rueda que el arroyo gira por ti: cómo mover más de lo que carga tu espalda.

yue.title: 路、轆同水
yue.intro: 雪橇、橋、車轆、船，仲有溪水幫你轉嘅水車——搬郁遠超你膊頭孭得起嘅嘢。

domains.title: Counting, Measuring and Writing
domains.intro: Marks that remember for you — numbers, honest measures, a calendar, and words that outlive the speaker.

guides.title: Count with tallies
guides.summary: Use notches and pebbles so you can count sheep, days, and sacks without holding them all in your head.

guides.title: Measure length, weight, and amounts
guides.summary: Agree on units — a stick, a balance with two pans, a marked cup — so 'a lot' becomes a number everyone trusts.

guides.title: Keep a calendar
guides.summary: Track the moon's faces and the sun's swing along the horizon so planting, harvest, and feasts come at the right time.

guides.title: Make ink, pens, and pages
guides.summary: Grind soot into ink, cut a reed or feather into a pen, and write on clay tablets, smooth bark, and boards.

guides.title: Write with an alphabet
guides.summary: Turn the sounds of speech into a small set of marks, and read the marks back into speech.

guides.title: Keep records and accounts
guides.summary: Write down harvests, stores, and debts so memory stops arguing with itself.

guides.title: Draw a map of your land
guides.summary: Pace out paths, water, and fields and draw them small, so plans can be made on a table.

guides.title: Make paper
guides.summary: Beat plant fiber into mush, lift a thin layer out on a screen, and press and dry it into a sheet.

zh.title: 数、量与书写
zh.intro: 替你记住事情的记号——数字、诚实的度量、历法，和比说话人活得更久的文字。

es.title: Contar, medir y escribir
es.intro: Marcas que recuerdan por ti: números, medidas honestas, un calendario y palabras que sobreviven a quien las dijo.

yue.title: 數數、量度同寫字
yue.intro: 幫你記住嘢嘅記號——數字、老實嘅度量衡、曆法，同埋比講嘢嗰個人活得更耐嘅文字。

domains.title: Living Together
domains.intro: Fair work, honest trade, shared joy, and rules everyone agreed to — the soft skills that turn houses into a village.

guides.title: Share work and food fairly
guides.summary: Split the jobs and the harvest in ways everyone can see are fair, and take turns at the jobs nobody loves.

guides.title: Trade what you have for what you need
guides.summary: Swap your extra for a neighbor's extra with honest measures and clear promises.

guides.title: Understand what money is
guides.summary: Hear the story of how tokens and coins grew out of trade and promises — and why trust is the real coin.

guides.title: Hold a council and decide together
guides.summary: Sit in a circle, let everyone speak in turn, decide as a group, and write the decision down.

guides.title: Settle disputes with agreed rules
guides.summary: Hear both sides all the way through before rules everyone agreed to, so quarrels end without wounds.

guides.title: Teach the young everything here
guides.summary: Pass every skill on by showing, telling, and letting them try — or the village lasts one lifetime.

guides.title: Tell stories, make music, hold feasts
guides.summary: Songs, games, rest days, and shared celebrations keep hands strong and hearts glad — a village that only works soon breaks.

guides.title: Build things everyone shares
guides.summary: Join hands on the well, the path, the bridge, and the meeting hall that no one could build alone.

guides.title: Plant a village
guides.summary: Put the whole book together — site, water, houses, fields, stores, and council — and start a place that outlives you.

zh.title: 共同生活
zh.intro: 公平的分工、诚实的交易、共享的欢乐，和大家都点头的规矩——把一堆房子变成一座村庄的软功夫。

es.title: Vivir juntos
es.intro: Trabajo justo, comercio honesto, alegría compartida y reglas acordadas por todos: lo que convierte casas en una aldea.

yue.title: 一齊生活
yue.intro: 公道嘅分工、老實嘅交易、共享嘅開心，同埋大家都點頭嘅規矩——將一堆屋變成一條村嘅軟功夫。
